notes: Scaffolds .ship/ with --global, --provider, --force. Works but no validation of existing state or conflict detection.
Core command. Activates agent, compiles immediately, --with for skill injection. Used by humans and agents. Needs better error messages on missing agent/skills.
Compiles active agent with --provider filter, --dry-run, --path. Well-tested via compiler surface.
Validates .ship/ config with --agent, --json, --path. Works but validation depth is limited — compiler input-validation spec (partial) covers the gaps.
Shows active agent and compilation status. Default command (no args). Simple and correct.
Decompiles provider configs into .ship/ format. Roundtrip tested for Claude/Cursor/Gemini but incomplete for Codex/OpenCode edge cases.
Resolves dependencies from ship.toml with --frozen lockfile check and --offline mode. Tested.
Publishes to registry with --dry-run and --tag. Validated server-side. CLI-side manifest validation is partial.
Adds package dependency or imports from Studio share link via --from. Works but error handling on bad URLs is weak.
Scans for hidden Unicode prompt injection vectors with --json output. Unique security tool. Well-scoped.
Login, logout, whoami for registry auth. Works but token refresh and expiry handling untested.
Get, set, list, path for ~/.ship/config.toml. Simple, correct, power user tool.
Built-in topic help system. Works.
Start, stop, status for shipd background process. Works but status detection is PID-based, no health check.
Launches Studio MCP HTTP server with --port and --open. Works but this is the Studio MCP being replaced by daemon.
Agent MCP sidecar entry point. stdio transport. Core infrastructure, used by every agent session.
add, add-stdio, list, remove for MCP server config. Works but overlaps with editing mcp.jsonc directly. Candidate for Studio GUI.
Hidden. Provider hook integration: session-start, before-tool, after-tool, session-end. Functional but undocumented.
Unstable. List events with --since, --actor, --entity, --action, --limit, --json. Depends on RT-01 event discipline. Power user debugging tool.
serve, status, stop for ship-network daemon. Overlaps with cmd-daemon. Unclear boundary — network serve may be subsumed into shipd.
YAGNI. list, create, edit, delete, clone, log — all trivially done with filesystem operations on .ship/agents/*.jsonc. Candidate for removal.
YAGNI. add, list, remove, create — overlaps with ship install for registry packages. Local operations are filesystem trivial. Candidate for removal.
YAGNI. set, get, append, reset for skill variable state. Admin GUI territory. No human types 'ship vars set commit-style gitmoji'. Candidate for removal.
Unstable. Real-time TUI dashboard for workspaces, sessions, events. Not v0.2.0.
